D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Indigo Leuco Vat — Reduction and Oxidation

Indigotin is water-insoluble; dyers reduce it to soluble yellow-green leuco form, soak the cloth, then air-oxidise back to blue on the fibre

The vat process is a redox shuttle. Reduce indigotin with sodium dithionite (modern) or fermented urine/woad (historic) → soluble leuco form penetrates fibre → atmospheric O₂ re-oxidises back to insoluble indigotin trapped inside the yarn.

Slide redox state: 0 = reduced leuco (yellow-green soluble) → 1 = oxidised indigotin (blue insoluble).
